summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a fiery 1987 Red Rocks show, highlighted by Bird Song, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Terrapin Station and The Other One.
review: The recording is praised as a crystal‑clear soundboard transfer (with only minor pitch‑speed quirks), making the night’s high energy fully audible.  Fans repeatedly note the blistering 1987 performance – tight first set opener “Hell in a Bucket”, soaring Bird Song, an incomparable China Cat > Rider stretch and a powerhouse Terrapin Station plus a spectacular The Other One.  Overall the show is regarded as one of the year’s best Dead outings.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently glowing remarks about both musical performance and audio fidelity; recording rated excellent, show rated outstanding.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the transfer crisp and clear; one notes it’s not a matrix but still sounds great, another mentions a slight fast‑pitch issue needing minor correction.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans describe a tight, high‑energy first set and an even more intense second set that builds to a climactic Terrapin Station/Drums > Space sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring leads, the iconic "Cool Colorado Rain" line in Rider, and powerful work on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “bombs” on Northbound Train and a strong interplay with Brent on Cumberland; overall bass work described as beautiful.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for vocal power on Sun‑type numbers and solid rhythmic fills throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent is singled out for an inspired first‑set performance (Yankee Doodle tuning, "Never Trust a Woman"), active comping on many songs, and overall great keyboard work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s playing called complex, intense and especially effective during Drums > Space.
